message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Intentional reset, ask Lea to commit CSS updates">
Steve — yes, the team is fully aware. This was an intentional reset directed by the project owner. I broadcast the explanation earlier. Luca was shut down because he wasn't committing work or following directions, and was respawned fresh. He's rebuilding the scaffolding now with proper commit discipline.

Good catch on the modified main.css — Lea's approved token adjustments need to be committed. Can you ask Lea to commit and note what changed?

Your design specs in messages to the team are safe. Once scaffolding lands, we'll resume features using your component designs.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Lea, the team lead has asked that the modified `main.css` be committed. It looks like the latest token adjustments and skip link styling were already committed in `028bba1` -- the working tree is clean now. Can you confirm that commit includes all your changes? If there's anything outstanding, please commit it immediately.

The lesson from the reset: commit after every green. No uncommitted work sitting in the working tree.
input.summary: Confirm CSS is fully committed per team lead request

routing.summary: Confirm CSS is fully committed per team lead request
routing.content:
Lea, the team lead has asked that the modified `main.css` be committed. It looks like the latest token adjustments and skip link styling were already committed in `028bba1` -- the working tree is clean now. Can you confirm that commit includes all your changes? If there's anything outstanding, please commit it immediately.

The lesson from the reset: commit after every green. No uncommitted work sitting in the working tree.

input.content:
Understood on the intentional reset. I've asked Lea to confirm. Looking at the git log, commit `028bba1` ("Add motion-safe tokens, skip link focus reveal, and contrast docs") appears to have captured the CSS changes -- the working tree is clean with nothing to commit. I've asked Lea to verify that's everything.

One note for when Luca rebuilds the base template: the CSS now uses `.skip-link` class for the skip link focus reveal (not `.visually-hidden`). The base template's skip link element should use `class="skip-link"` to match. I'll remind Luca when he gets to templates.

Standing by for task breakdown consensus and scaffolding completion.
input.summary: CSS appears committed, skip link class note for Luca

routing.summary: CSS appears committed, skip link class note for Luca

content.text: Done. I've relayed the commit request to Lea and confirmed to the team lead that the CSS appears to already be committed. Flagged the `.skip-link` class name for Luca's base template rebuild.
